description_long: This object is a vintage biplane with a wooden and metal frame, featuring two stacked wings with roundels painted on the upper surfaces. The fuselage is painted blue with a visible open cockpit, and a machine gun is mounted in front of the pilot's seat. The tail section is supported by a lightweight truss structure and features a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft, with exposed structural elements and a propeller at the nose.
description: A vintage biplane with stacked wings, open cockpit, blue fuselage, and fixed landing gear, featuring roundels and a front-mounted propeller.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings with roundels, open cockpit, and nose-mounted propeller.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the blue fuselage, open cockpit, machine gun, and the truss-supported tail section.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the alignment of the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the truss tail structure, cockpit, and the fixed landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, no winglets, and minimal dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit with a small windscreen.
